```python
expected_peaks = set(metadata["expected_peaks"])
expected_period = metadata["expected_period"]
n_qubits = len(metadata["expected_peaks"][0])
dimension = 1 << n_qubits

top_outcomes = sorted(counts, key=counts.get, reverse=True)[:2]

assert len(top_outcomes) > 0, "No outcomes observed"
for outcome in top_outcomes:
    assert outcome in expected_peaks, f"Outcome {outcome} is not an expected peak"
    val = int(outcome, 2)
    assert (val * expected_period) % dimension == 0, f"Outcome {outcome} does not yield period {expected_period}"
```

```python
assert len(counts) > 0, "Counts dictionary is empty"

total_weight = sum(counts.values())
assert total_weight > 0, "Total count weight is zero"

# Identify the most frequent outcome (observed peak)
max_key = max(counts, key=counts.get)
max_val = counts[max_key]

# Verify the observed peak is among the expected peaks from metadata
expected_peaks = metadata["expected_peaks"]
assert max_key in expected_peaks, f"Observed peak {max_key} is not in expected peaks {expected_peaks}"

# Verify the observed peak leads to the expected period via continued-fraction logic
# The circuit uses 3 qubits, so the denominator N = 2^3 = 8
N = 8
y = int(max_key, 2)
a, b = y, N
while b:
    a, b = b, a % b
gcd_val = a
derived_period = N // gcd_val

expected_period = metadata["expected_period"]
assert derived_period == expected_period, f"Derived period {derived_period} does not match expected {expected_period}"

# Verify the peak dominates the spectrum (signal-to-noise check)
assert max_val > total_weight * 0.3, "Observed peak does not dominate the spectrum"
```

```python
assert len(counts) > 0, "Counts dictionary is empty"

# Retrieve target period and infer register size from metadata
target_period = metadata["expected_period"]
expected_peaks = metadata["expected_peaks"]
assert len(expected_peaks) > 0, "Expected peaks list is empty"
n = len(expected_peaks[0])
total_states = 1 << n

# Identify the most frequent outcome (dominant peak)
top_peak = max(counts, key=counts.get)
y = int(top_peak, 2)

# Compute GCD(y, 2^n) inline to determine the denominator of the fraction y/2^n
# The period r is approximated by the denominator of the convergent of y/2^n
# For exact peaks in this simplified spectrum, r = 2^n / gcd(y, 2^n)
a, b = total_states, y
while b:
    a, b = b, a % b
gcd_val = a

recovered_period = total_states // gcd_val

# Verify the recovered period matches the expected period
assert recovered_period == target_period, f"Recovered period {recovered_period} does not match expected {target_period}"
```

```python
expected_peaks = set(metadata["expected_peaks"])
expected_period = metadata["expected_period"]
n_qubits = len(metadata["expected_peaks"][0])
N = 1 << n_qubits

# Identify the most frequent outcomes
sorted_counts = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
top_keys = [k for k, v in sorted_counts[:2]]

# Verify observed peaks match the expected set
assert len(top_keys) > 0, "No outcomes observed"
assert all(k in expected_peaks for k in top_keys), f"Observed peaks {top_keys} do not match expected {expected_peaks}"

# Verify peaks lead to recovering the expected period via continued-fraction logic
for key in top_keys:
    val = int(key, 2)
    assert val != 0, "Peak value 0 cannot recover period"
    # Compute gcd(val, N) inline
    a, b = val, N
    while b:
        a, b = b, a % b
    gcd_val = a
    period = N // gcd_val
    assert period == expected_period, f"Recovered period {period} != {expected_period}"
```
